danger_signals.visual_form: Automatic weapon sight lock onto nearby enemies; sight changes shape to indicate threat
Red health bar on left side of screen decreasing; eventual visual distortion as sanity depletes
danger_signals.meaning: Alice taking psychic/physical damage; game state deteriorating

danger_signals.visual_form: Platforming obstacles, traps, unstable terrain; visual cues indicating fall zones
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial threats requiring avoidance or platforming skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Twisted, grotesque environment design; dark color palette; corrupted familiar elements
danger_signals.meaning: World corrupted by Alice's psychological damage; inherent danger from broken reality

danger_signals.visual_form: Blue bar on right side of screen depleting; weapon malfunction when too low
danger_signals.meaning: Magic resource exhaustion; inability to use ranged/special weapons

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible crystal objects scattered throughout levels; some respawn in specific locations
safety_signals.meaning: Sanity restoration available; Wonderland's life force providing recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Materials dropped after defeating enemies; volume scales with enemy difficulty
safety_signals.meaning: Imagination power available for restoration; defeating threats yields resources

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC character model; appears as guide figure
safety_signals.meaning: Ally presence; guidance and narrative support available

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon sight transforms into footprint indicators showing safe landing surfaces
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation is possible; safe platforming path identified

safety_signals.visual_form: Rare items with distinctive visual appearance (Ragebox Elixir, Darkened Looking Glass, Grasshopper Tea); alter Alice's appearance when active
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary power-ups available; enhanced capabilities for limited duration

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Queen of Hearts iconography; royal/authoritarian symbols
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies serving the Queen; corrupted Wonderland creatures and soldiers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress of Doors; royal architecture; areas of active oppression and decline; despondent settlements
Vale of Tears; gnome hideouts; villages seeking aid; areas promised liberation

faction_indicators.faction: Wonderland Commons (Mock Turtle, gnomes, village inhabitants)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Neutral NPC characters; traditional Wonderland denizens
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Villages; schools; common areas showing signs of decline under Queen's rule

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Queen of Hearts in control / Wonderland declining
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Disfigured, gloomy environment; twisted architecture; dark color palette; grotesque creature designs; corrupted familiar elements from Carroll source material
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively hostile; NPCs despondent; inhabitants seeking champion; White Rabbit in hiding/resistance mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wonderland appears broken and cruel rather than whimsical; impossible geometry; nightmarish versions of familiar locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Alice mute or communicating through action; reliance on 'toys' as combat tools; sanity meter as primary health indicator

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sanity bar depleting visually on left side of screen; possible visual distortion/screen effects as sanity approaches zero
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game premature end when sanity fully depleted; forced respawn/reload

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon acquisition (Vorpal Blade, toys); size alteration via gnome elixir; temporary enhancement items visually alter Alice's appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat capability increasing; access to new areas; NPC assistance (gnome aid, Mock Turtle quest); progression through Fortress of Doors

environmental_storytelling.example: Fortress of Doors represents authoritarian control; school inside shows indoctrination/oppression systems; design conveys Queen's grip on institutions
Villages appear despondent under Queen's rule; inhabitants clearly showing signs of despair; environmental clues indicating resource scarcity or oppression
Familiar Carroll elements (tea parties, rabbit holes, looking glass) reappear as twisted/nightmarish versions; conveys Alice's psychological distortion of beloved memories
Platforming challenges, traps, and obstacles tell story of world becoming hostile; increasing difficulty reflects Alice's deeper psychological descent
Enhancement items visibly alter Alice's appearance; temporary power-ups show as visual modifications (dress becomes parachute, etc.); communicates magical state changes
White Rabbit remains elusive/unreachable initially; gnomes in hiding; Mock Turtle seeking aid—all indicate resistance status and hidden allies

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Combat-capable; can use all toys at full effectiveness; responsive to environmental interaction; can solve puzzles and navigate complex platforming
Reduced effectiveness; eventual inability to continue; game prematurely ends at zero sanity
Ranged/special weapons malfunction; forced to rely on Vorpal Blade melee combat; restricted tactical options
Altered appearance; temporary increased damage (Ragebox), invisibility (Looking Glass), or speed/jump height (Grasshopper Tea); limited duration behavior change
Engage Alice in combat; weapon sight auto-locks; threat behavior triggers
Drop meta-substance loot; volume scales with enemy strength; provide resource recovery opportunity
Maintains Wonderland in decline and despondency; controls Fortress infrastructure; oppresses inhabitants; blocks White Rabbit's promised champion
Promises Alice as champion; remains elusive initially; represents hope/liberation narrative thread
Invites Alice to follow White Rabbit; provides narrative direction; acts as ally/narrator presence

notes: American McGee's Alice uses a heavily stylized dark fantasy aesthetic to communicate a world corrupted by the protagonist's psychological trauma. The visual language relies on juxtaposition: familiar Carroll elements rendered grotesque and hostile. The UI directly represents mental state (sanity/willpower bars) rather than traditional health, making the manifestation layer explicitly tied to psychological condition. Faction communication is implicit through environment and NPC interactions rather than explicit visual markers—the Queen's oppression is shown through architectural control and NPC despair, while resistance is signaled through the White Rabbit's promise and hidden allies. The game's linearity and heavy platforming serve the narrative: Alice is psychologically constrained and must navigate through her own fractured perceptions.
